module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands — 8+/10 per teaching beat; below 7 = REVISE.
rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[0].mood_score=8, trace.close.mood_score=8. Single teaching phase (lecture has no ## Phase headers). Both the arc-recognition beat (Para 1) and the reframe beat (Para 2) are TEACHING beats — engagement IS the pedagogical move — so mood-floor applies and both clear 8. No beat below 8. Mood-floor carve-out for setup beats not applicable: lecture has no mechanical setup steps.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — check module N+1 teaching moment.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture is placed EARLY in M6, before the main exercise. The practitioner-fluency mood is primed by the closing question ('How do you know it's right?') but deliberately not resolved — maintainer block instructs 'Let it sit. Don't answer it for the room.' The exercise and Debrief carry the resolution. No M7/M8 teaching moments are pre-empted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands — reliably triggers across reasonable persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment: spec-and-tests → grounding reframe (Para 2). The Agentics Helsinki anecdote (first meetup, fall 2025, single dev, 500K lines, recurring discipline of spec-and-tests) provides concrete grounding for the abstract pivot. For a 2-week Claude Code engineer who has lived M1–M5, the recognition (Para 1) and reframe (Para 2) land reliably. Trace confusion flags are minor (brief ambiguity about whether tests are being devalued, resolved within the same paragraph) and do not undercut the teaching moment. No under-scaffolded phases.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — file honors module Big Idea and mood contract.
rules_evaluated.evidence: M6 Big Idea per strategy doc: encoding as the third phase of test→learn→encode; practitioner fluency as the felt payoff. This lecture names the quality arc threaded across the whole training and reframes quality as grounding — establishing the WHY for M6's encode move. The open closing question ('How do you know it's right?') hands off to the exercise without pre-empting it. Big Idea alignment present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Para 1 names the quality arc the student just lived through (M1 tests-first → M2 plan push-back → M3 skill → M5 verifier → M6 second skill). Placement diagnostic: 'naming a pattern = closer.' Lecture is placed EARLY in M6, before the main exercise. Rule: 'Told before = abstract claim. Told after = recognition.' The pattern-recognition content (Para 1) belongs in a closer; the stakes-setting function and reframe (Para 2 + closing question) could survive as an opener if stripped of retrospective arc-naming.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Move Para 1's arc-summary to after the main exercise, or reframe it as ceiling-showing ('here's where you stand going into this') rather than pattern-naming ('here's the pattern you just lived') to shift from closer to opener register.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Rule targets contrast-mood modules (M5-style: learn through doing, name it after). M6's mood contract is practitioner fluency (leverage register), not contrast/unease. This lecture is placed before M6's main exercise, which is a leverage-mood exercise, not a contrast-mood exercise. Rule does not apply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture has no fenced prompts. No forcing-function elements in body prose. Lectures don't carry fenced prompts; this rule's enforcement surface is absent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages room outside text — no authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'discuss with...', 'share with your neighbor', 'tell the room', pair-share, or pause-cue sentences in the body. The closing question ('How do you know it's right?') is a rhetorical/epistemic hand-off to the exercise, not a cohort-management cue. Body is entirely narrative/teaching register throughout.

Closing question ('How do you know it's right?') is genuinely epistemic — open, not accusatory. The reframe from spec-and-tests to grounding adds a dimension rather than correcting a failure. No 'you did it wrong' or 'having-done-wrong' register. Para 2's implicit failure mode (shipping at scale without grounding) appears only as a rhetorical question at the close, not as the lecture's framing lead.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture leads with 'The quality arc started at M1 with tests-first' — a positive discipline named first. 'Grounding' is introduced as a positive extension. The failure-mode framing ('How do you know it's right?') appears only at the close as a rhetorical question, not as the lecture's title or lead.
